content: Instead of framing reinforcement learning (RL) reward optimization as a general search over action spaces, we can treat an RL agent’s reward signal as an active learning query to its own memory: the agent only explicitly stores and updates trajectories that actively reduce uncertainty about long-horizon reward maxima, rather than caching every interaction. This creates a sparse, uncertainty-guided memory architecture that outperforms traditional uniform replay buffers for long-horizon RL tasks by aligning memory allocation with the core goal of reward maximization.
